notes: Scare quotes / term-of-art, not an attributed quotation: quoted span is a NAME for something (a way of saying), no speaker, no attribution verb. speaker_name:null was the available signal. Registry needs a negative indicator separating speech quotes from scare quotes (short span + no attribution context + often title-case term).

Not speech — this is a MISSION STATEMENT quoted inline (…its overall mission "to improve the health…"). speaker_name:null again. Two registry actions: (1) quotation negative indicator: quoted span introduced by a naming phrase (its mission / known as / so-called / titled) is not an attributed quote; (2) NEW pattern type worth adding: mission_statement (Layer 1) — lead-in contains mission/vision/values + org as subject; component candidates: mission callout / about-block. Second speaker_name:null false positive today — absence of a speaker should already damp quotation confidence.
this is a year, not a statistic
speaker is Patricia Samra
speaker is Patricia Samra
speaker is Patricia Samra
the Q&A pattern relies on having answers.  This is a rhetorical question that leads into a discussion, with no specific answer given.
this is a mission statement.  note it's called their "vision" instead of missino.
while "springfield" is a place, in this context it's the name of an employer - MGM Springfield.  and there's not enough context to know what state springfield is.  so this is an entity not a place.
fantastic pattern!  Funding!  [entity] gave [entity] [dollar amount] in funding for [ purpose]. I WANT
relationship
relationship
this is a straight citation / footnotes.  we have those already identified and could mine them if we wish.  but it'll be very noisy with dates and entities.
citation / footnote
citation / footnote
citation / footnote
this is a longer quotation, cut in the middle by the name of the speaker. very common pattern.
part two of the earlier multi-piece quote
quotes indicate a title of a thing
we should skip over citation / footnote.
we should skip over citation / footnote.
we should skip over citation / footnote.
COVID-19 is not a stat
i think funding should specifically not be a statistic.
funding should specifically not be a statistic
ok, but "this model" is nebulous and had to quantify as the label of the stat.

I'm torn.  stats should be generalized and cover a larger concern than one individual's number of training hours.  OK this is a "storytelling callout". It's talks about somebody doing a thing, instead of "x% of Y have Z"
EEO-14 is a proper name of a thing
storytelling: this date pertains to a single person's experience
rhetorical
rhetorical

CITATION-ZONE bulk reject: the atom is correctly detected but lives inside source-note/citation text — a zone exclusion, not a detector error. Rule: content-pattern mining should skip footnote/citation zones (our IR marks them explicitly; refs + footnotes nodes). Exclude these from per-type acceptance rates or segment stats by zone.
